name: microsoft-todo-intray
description: Read, append to, or remove items in the user's Microsoft To Do in-tray — a single Microsoft To Do list used for quick capture. Use this whenever the user wants to capture a task or note to their in-tray, check what is sitting in their in-tray, or clear an item from it. Trigger on phrases like "add this to my in-tray", "capture this", "put it in Microsoft To Do", "what's in my in-tray", "clear that from my in-tray", or any request to dump, review, or clear quick-capture tasks — even if they never say the word "in-tray". Only ever touches the one configured list, never any other Microsoft To Do list or any other part of the account.
instructions:
# Microsoft To Do — In-tray

Manages ONE Microsoft To Do list (the user's in-tray) via the Microsoft Graph API.
Every command is hard-scoped to a single list id; this skill cannot read or write
any other list.

This skill is **full read AND write**: it can list, add, complete, and delete items in
the in-tray when the user asks. (Note: the daily-brief skill runs in propose-only mode and
will not auto-modify the in-tray during a brief, but that is the brief's own restraint, not
a limit on this skill.)

The Microsoft refresh token is stored in a private GitHub gist, not in the skill.
Each run reads the current token, uses it, and writes back the fresh token Microsoft
returns, so it self-renews and normally never needs manual re-minting.

## When to use
- The user wants to **capture** a task/note into the in-tray → `add`
- The user asks **what's in** the in-tray → `list`
- The user processed an item and wants it **gone** → `delete` (hard remove) or `done` (mark completed)

## Commands

**The script and `config.json` already live in this folder, configured. Just run it in place; nothing needs setting up.**

```
python3 /mnt/skills/user/microsoft-todo-intray/scripts/intray.py list
python3 /mnt/skills/user/microsoft-todo-intray/scripts/intray.py add "Buy oat milk"
python3 /mnt/skills/user/microsoft-todo-intray/scripts/intray.py delete <task_id>
python3 /mnt/skills/user/microsoft-todo-intray/scripts/intray.py done <task_id>
```

Resolves config via `__file__`, HTTP-only, so `/mnt` being read-only is irrelevant. Don't check `/home/claude` (empty every fresh session) and don't copy the files. That false copy step is what causes the bogus "not set up" read.

`list` prints each open item with its id. To delete or complete an item, pass the
id shown by `list`. When the user refers to an item by number ("clear item 2"),
run `list` first to map the number to its id, then act.

## Reporting back to the user
Relay results plainly — confirm what was added, show the in-tray contents as a short
list, or confirm the removal. Don't dump raw JSON.

## If something fails
- **Network error reaching Microsoft** → add `login.microsoftonline.com` and
  `graph.microsoft.com` to code-execution allowed domains.
- **Network error reaching GitHub** → add `api.github.com` to code-execution allowed domains.
- **Expired/invalid refresh token (Microsoft error)** → token went unused past its window
  or was revoked (e.g. password change). Re-run `scripts/get_token.py` to reseed the gist.
- **GitHub gist API error** → `github_token` is missing the `gist` scope, or `gist_id` is wrong.
- **Missing config value(s)** → `config.json` isn't filled in. Point them to SETUP.md.

## Configuration & safety
- `config.json` holds `client_id`, `list_id`, `github_token` (classic PAT, `gist` scope),
  and `gist_id`. The Microsoft refresh token itself lives in the gist. Setup is in SETUP.md.
- The Microsoft token grants access to ALL the user's To Do lists; the single-list limit is
  enforced only by this script (it never addresses another list id).
- Never print or echo the refresh token or the GitHub token.
